query: How is retrieval quality measured in RAG systems?
final_answer: Retrieval quality in RAG systems is measured using key metrics such as Precision@K, Recall@K, and Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR). Precision@K measures the fraction of retrieved documents that are relevant, Recall@K measures the fraction of relevant documents that are retrieved, and MRR measures the rank of the first relevant document.
